text:
REGIME — Risk-on relief rally, tech led, energy diverged lower.

INDICES — VIX 25.2, falling. IWM +3.50%, QQQ +3.39%, SPY +2.91%, DIA +2.46%; small caps beat large caps today. Silver surged +7.27%, the sharpest cross-asset move, with VIXY (VIX futures) collapsing -9.32%.

SECTORS — XLK +4.24%, XLI +3.27%, XLY +3.14%, XLC +2.69%, XLF +2.09%, XLV +1.94%, XLB +1.79%, XLRE +1.54%, XLP +0.12%, XLU -0.07%, XLE -1.13%. Top industries: MSOS +12.70% and SILJ +8.82%; bottom: XOP -1.97% and USO -1.99%.

THE 7s — Fab 7 +6.22%, Old Metals 7 +5.85%, Semi 7 +5.75%, Buildout 7 +5.41%, New Metals 7 +5.02%, Cloud 7 +4.68%, Bank 7 +3.98%, New Staples 7 +3.82%, Industrial 7 +3.70%, Freight 7 +2.94%, Defense 7 +2.29%, Housing 7 +2.17%, Health 7 +2.16%, Software 7 +1.88%, Medtech 7 +1.37%, Toll Road 7 +0.85%, Insurance 7 +0.81%, Old Staples 7 -0.31%, Power 7 -0.59%, Energy 7 -1.45%. Strongest ticker in Fab 7 not specified in data; weakest in Energy 7 unspecified, though XOP -1.97% anchored the group.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 50.3, Neutral. Top movers: MSOS +12.70% (Industries), SILJ +8.82% (Industries), SLV +7.27% (Cross-Asset). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +9.09%, worst Housing 7 -10.80%; best basket Energy 7 +11.21%, worst Old Metals 7 -13.50%. Brent crude's -14.63 drop drove XLE -1.13% despite a broad risk-on tape.

WATCH — PPH: +1.94%, 13.6x average volume — massive vol spike with muted price response warrants scrutiny. DBC: -1.06%, 4.0x volume, diverging from equity rally amid commodity pressure. ARGT: +4.75%, 2.6x volume — Argentina-specific catalyst possible; trend divergence from EM peers worth monitoring.
